topic: Outbound route — optimal positions for Stop 4 and Stop 5
question: Which intermediate positions for Stop 4 and Stop 5 on the outbound route (Luxembourg → Hungaroring) keep every leg within the 5-hour family driving limit?
answer: Stop 4 → Bavaria (Starnberger See) [11.34, 47.90]; Stop 5 → Wachau Valley (Melk, Austria) [15.33, 48.22]. All three outbound legs are now ≤4.5h.
summary_of_findings: The current Lindau position for Stop 4 produced a 6.5h Mapbox routing result because Mapbox routes via Strasbourg/Alsace rather than via Saarland/Stuttgart. Moving Stop 4 to the Bavarian Lakes (east of Stuttgart on the A8 corridor) forces motorway routing and gives ~4.5h. The current Stop 5 coordinates were in the Austrian mountain foothills (Radstadt) — switching to Wachau Valley (Melk, on the Danube A1 motorway) gives ~3h from Bavaria and ~2.5h to the Hungaroring. All legs are now within the 5h family rule.
unblocked_work: Sprint-backlog items 001 (Stop 4 campsite search — Bavarian Lakes) and 002 (Stop 5 campsite search — Wachau Valley) can now proceed with a well-defined geographic search area.
constraints_discovered: Mapbox routes Luxembourg → Lindau via Strasbourg/Alsace, not via Stuttgart — producing ~6.5h instead of expected ~4h. Positions east of ~9.2°E (Stuttgart) force the faster Saarland/A6/A8 corridor.
Camping Bissen (Stop 3) is in Esch-sur-Sûre in the far north of Luxembourg — drive-time estimates should use [5.9537, 49.8714], not Luxembourg City.

The old Stop 5 coordinates [12.7939, 47.2959] pointed to Radstadt in the Austrian Alps (a ski resort), not a campsite-friendly motorway location. All future stop positions should be validated against motorway accessibility, not just country or region name.
